Bash(git -C /opt/restaurant-system commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
fix\(hub\): Save vendor_id and item_code in expense item mappings

Bulk mapping endpoint was not storing vendor_id or item_code in the
invoice_item_mapping table, causing the Expense Items page to show
"-" for vendor and item code columns. Also added item_code search
to the expense items filter.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git -C /opt/restaurant-system commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
fix\(hub\): Use vendor purchase_unit_abbr for Unit column on invoice detail

The template was using units_per_case > 1 to show CS, else EA. This was
wrong for items like Hendricks Gin \(sold per bottle but 6 per case\) which
showed CS instead of EA. Now uses the vendor item''s purchase_unit_abbr
which correctly reflects the actual purchasing unit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git -C /opt/restaurant-system commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
fix\(hub\): Use price_is_per_unit flag in location price display

The location-prices endpoint assumed invoice unit_price was always a
case cost, causing items priced per unit \(like Hendricks Gin at
$38.20/bottle\) to show $38.20 as case cost and $6.37 as unit cost.
Now uses the price_is_per_unit flag to correctly calculate: per-unit
items show unit_cost = invoice price, case_cost = price * units_per_case.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(__NEW_LINE_54e89065e86d797b__ docker compose exec -T inventory-app python3 -c "
import httpx
# Check if there are old sheets/templates from master items era
r = httpx.get\(''http://localhost:8000/api/order-sheets/?limit=10'', cookies={''access_token'': ''$TOKEN''}\)
sheets = r.json\(\)
print\(f''Existing order sheets: {len\(sheets\)}''\)
for s in sheets:
    print\(f''  Sheet {s[""id""]}: {s[""name""]} \(status={s[""status""]}\)''\)

r = httpx.get\(''http://localhost:8000/api/order-sheet-templates/?active_only=false'', cookies={''access_token'': ''$TOKEN''}\)
templates = r.json\(\)
print\(f''Existing templates: {len\(templates\)}''\)
for t in templates:
    print\(f''  Template {t[""id""]}: {t[""name""]} \({t[""item_count""]} items\)''\)
")

Bash(__NEW_LINE_871fe946414b46ea__ docker compose exec -T inventory-app python3 -c "
import httpx, json

# Create template with integer par levels
data = {
    ''location_id'': 1,
    ''name'': ''Test Integer Pars'',
    ''items'': [
        {
            ''hub_vendor_item_id'': 499,
            ''par_level'': 5,
            ''sort_order'': 0,
            ''item_name'': ''Goslings Ginger Beer'',
            ''vendor_sku'': ''000920435'',
            ''vendor_name'': ''Southern Glaziers'',
            ''category'': ''NAB'',
            ''unit_abbr'': ''EA''
        },
        {
            ''hub_vendor_item_id'': 19,
            ''par_level'': 10,
            ''sort_order'': 1,
            ''item_name'': ''Modelo Especial 16oz'',
            ''vendor_sku'': ''8585'',
            ''vendor_name'': ''Double Eagle'',
            ''category'': ''Beer'',
            ''unit_abbr'': ''CS''
        }
    ]
}

# Create template
r = httpx.post\(''http://localhost:8000/api/order-sheet-templates/'', cookies={''access_token'': ''$TOKEN''}, json=data\)
print\(f''Create template: {r.status_code}''\)
tmpl = r.json\(\)
tmpl_id = tmpl[''id'']
print\(f''  Template {tmpl_id}: {tmpl[""name""]}''\)
for item in tmpl[''items'']:
    print\(f''    {item[""item_name""]}: par={item[""par_level""]} \(type={type\(item[""par_level""]\).__name__}\)''\)

# Create order sheet
r = httpx.post\(''http://localhost:8000/api/order-sheets/'', cookies={''access_token'': ''$TOKEN''}, json={''template_id'': tmpl_id}\)
print\(f''\\nCreate order sheet: {r.status_code}''\)
sheet = r.json\(\)
sheet_id = sheet[''id'']
print\(f''  Sheet {sheet_id}: {sheet[""name""]}''\)

# Enter on-hand values \(integers\)
items_update = [{''id'': item[''id''], ''on_hand'': 3} for item in sheet[''items'']]
r = httpx.put\(f''http://localhost:8000/api/order-sheets/{sheet_id}'', cookies={''access_token'': ''$TOKEN''}, json={''items'': items_update}\)
print\(f''\\nUpdate on-hand: {r.status_code}''\)
result = r.json\(\)
for item in result[''items'']:
    print\(f''  {item[""item_name""]}: par={item[""par_level""]}, on_hand={item[""on_hand""]}, to_order={item[""to_order""]} \(types: {type\(item[""par_level""]\).__name__}/{type\(item[""on_hand""]\).__name__}/{type\(item[""to_order""]\).__name__}\)''\)

# Cleanup
r = httpx.delete\(f''http://localhost:8000/api/order-sheets/{sheet_id}'', cookies={''access_token'': ''$TOKEN''}\)
print\(f''\\nDelete sheet: {r.status_code}''\)
r = httpx.delete\(f''http://localhost:8000/api/order-sheet-templates/{tmpl_id}'', cookies={''access_token'': ''$TOKEN''}\)
print\(f''Delete template: {r.status_code}''\)
")

Bash(/dev/null <<'EOF'
SELECT vi.id, left\(vi.vendor_product_name, 50\) as name, vi.units_per_case::numeric\(10,0\) as upc, vi.case_cost,
       u.abbreviation as uom, viuom.conversion_factor::numeric\(10,0\) as cf, viuom.is_default as dflt, viuom.last_cost
FROM hub_vendor_items vi
JOIN vendor_item_uoms viuom ON viuom.vendor_item_id = vi.id
JOIN units_of_measure u ON u.id = viuom.uom_id
WHERE vi.vendor_id = \(SELECT id FROM hub_vendors WHERE vendor_name ILIKE '%bj%' LIMIT 1\)
ORDER BY vi.id, viuom.is_default DESC;
EOF)
